data-01-planning-data-fundamentals.description: Understand what data-driven planning means and how data flows through the Planning software to produce a plan.

data-02-data-quality-and-impact.description: Understand what good data looks like, where data problems come from, and how to build practices that keep planning data healthy over time.

data-03-data-types.description: Master data, transactional data, and planning parameters are three distinct data categories. Understanding the difference — and where each lives — is essential for data quality management.

data-04-data-governance.title: Data Governance
data-04-data-governance.description: Data confidence, ownership, definitions, and the single source of truth — the governance structures that keep planning data reliable across a multi-system landscape.

data-05-data-sources-and-model.title: Data Sources & Architecture
data-05-data-sources-and-model.description: Where planning data originates and how the Planning software represents the supply chain as a connected graph of nodes and relationships.

data-literacy.title: Data Literacy
data-literacy.description: Understanding data well enough to plan with confidence — reading outputs, questioning assumptions, and spotting errors.

data-validation-routines.title: Data Validation Routines
data-validation-routines.description: The checks and routines used to validate planning data before it enters the plan.

decision-flow-overview.title: Decision Flow Overview
decision-flow-overview.description: How decisions flow from strategic to operational — the decision architecture across planning horizons.

decision-frameworks.title: Decision Frameworks
decision-frameworks.description: The frameworks planners use to structure complex decisions — trade-off tools, criteria hierarchies, and consensus models.

decision-making-skills.title: Decision-Making Skills
decision-making-skills.description: How effective planners make decisions under uncertainty — structured thinking, bias awareness, and trade-off clarity.

demand-and-forecast-data.title: Demand & Forecast Data
demand-and-forecast-data.description: The data that drives demand planning — statistical inputs, commercial overlays, consensus signals, and real-time demand signals including orders, bookings, and market intelligence.

demand-planner.title: Demand Planner
demand-planner.description: Role profile for the Demand Planner — responsibilities, day-to-day tasks, and interactions across S&OP and S&OE.

erp-01-erp-basics.description: How ERP works internally — its evolution from legacy systems, the core concepts that govern transaction processing, and the structural assumptions that downstream planning depends on. For a high-level introduction to what ERP is and why it exists, see The Four Systems in the Tool Landscape module.

erp-02-the-data-model.title: ERP Data Model
erp-02-the-data-model.description: How ERP organises data into items, locations, and transactions — the structures that planning relies on to execute confirmed orders.

erp-03-data-flow-into-erp.description: What operational data enters ERP, why it matters for planning, and where each data type originates — covering manual entry, system-generated transactions, and integration feeds. For the technical integration mechanics, see How the Systems Connect in the Tool Landscape module.

erp-04-data-flow-out-of-erp.title: Data Flow: Out of ERP

erp-05-the-logic.title: How ERP Processes Transactions
erp-05-the-logic.description: The business rules that govern how ERP processes orders, manages inventory, and posts transactions — what happens automatically and why.

erp-06-key-erp-workflows.title: Key ERP Workflows
erp-06-key-erp-workflows.description: The core transactions planners trigger or monitor in ERP — releasing orders, receiving goods, issuing materials, and extracting reports.

erp-07-navigation-and-ui.title: Navigating ERP
erp-07-navigation-and-ui.description: How to move around ERP efficiently — the interface structure, search, filters, and the key pages planners return to every day.

escalation-behaviour.title: Escalation Behaviour

exec-01-execution-fundamentals.title: Execution Fundamentals

exec-actuals-capture.title: Actuals Capture
exec-actuals-capture.description: Recording what actually happened — production completions, shipments, receipts, and stock movements — into ERP and FMS so the planning system has accurate starting positions.

exec-execution-monitoring.title: Execution Monitoring
exec-execution-monitoring.description: Tracking daily operational performance against the short-term plan — production throughput, shipment status, and inventory movements — and flagging deviations in time to act.

exec-feedback-to-planning.title: Feedback to Planning
exec-feedback-to-planning.description: Closing the loop between execution and planning — surfacing systematic variances, flagging constraint changes, and triggering plan updates where execution reality has diverged from the plan.

exec-order-prioritisation.title: Order Prioritisation
exec-order-prioritisation.description: Applying agreed prioritisation rules to sequence open orders when supply is constrained — ensuring the right customers and products are served first.

faq-and-troubleshooting.title: FAQ and Troubleshooting
faq-and-troubleshooting.description: Answers to the most common questions about tool usage — and how to resolve the most frequent issues.

finance.title: Finance

fms-01-understanding-basics.title: FMS Basics

For a high-level introduction to what FMS is and why it exists, see The Four Systems in the Tool Landscape module.

fms-04-data-flow-out-of-fms.title: What FMS Sends to Planning

fms-mdm-in-the-landscape.description: Why FMS and MDM are grouped as supporting systems, how they differ from each other, and how they interact — the context needed before diving into either system's detail.

forecast-assumptions.title: Forecast Assumptions
forecast-assumptions.description: The commercial and statistical assumptions built into the demand forecast — growth rates, seasonality, and market conditions.

integrated-planning-concept.title: Integrated Planning Concept
integrated-planning-concept.description: How S&OP, S&OE, and Execution form one integrated planning system — the logic, the hand-offs, and the dependencies.

inventory-data.title: Inventory Data
inventory-data.description: Stock positions, coverage calculations, safety stock parameters, and real-time inventory health signals — coverage flags, shortfall alerts, and excess indicators.

kpi-definitions.title: KPI Definitions
kpi-definitions.description: Precise definitions of each planning KPI — what it measures, how it is calculated, and what good looks like.

kpi-interpretation.title: KPI Interpretation
kpi-interpretation.description: How to read planning KPIs in context — trend analysis, benchmarking, and avoiding misleading conclusions.

lifecycle-data.title: Lifecycle Data
lifecycle-data.description: New product introductions, phase-outs, and lifecycle status — the data that tracks what is active in the plan.

material-planning.title: Material Planning
material-planning.description: MRP and material requirements — translating the production schedule into component and raw material needs.

mdm-01-understanding-basics.title: MDM Basics
mdm-01-understanding-basics.description: How MDM works internally — what master data is, why data management matters across the system landscape, and the core concepts that underpin how MDM serves ERP, Planning software, and FMS. For a high-level introduction to what MDM is and why it exists, see The Four Systems in the Tool Landscape module.

mdm-02-the-data-model.title: MDM Data Model
mdm-02-the-data-model.description: How MDM organises product data — item hierarchies, attribute structures, and the relationships between entities that downstream systems rely on.

mdm-03-data-flow-into-mdm.title: Data Flow: Into MDM
mdm-03-data-flow-into-mdm.description: Where master data originates and how it enters MDM — from product creation requests and supplier updates to system-generated changes — before it is distributed to Planning software, ERP, and FMS.

mdm-04-data-flow-out-of-mdm.title: What MDM Sends to Planning
mdm-04-data-flow-out-of-mdm.description: How MDM distributes master data to Planning software, ERP, FMS, and other systems — and what to do when systems fall out of sync. For the technical integration mechanics, see How the Systems Connect in the Tool Landscape module.

mdm-logic-and-workflows.title: MDM Logic & Workflows
mdm-logic-and-workflows.description: How MDM governs master data and manages record changes — the governance rules and approval workflows that maintain data quality, plus the core workflows for creating, updating, and auditing records.

operations.description: How Operations engages in supply review and execution — capacity ownership, constraint escalation, and feedback to planning.

people-02-accountability.description: How responsibility is assigned across the S&OP cycle — who owns each step, who supports, and who needs to be kept informed.

people-03-planning-cadences.title: Planning Cadences
people-03-planning-cadences.description: A week, month, and quarter in the life of the three planning horizons — what each planner does, when, and why.

performance-dashboards.description: How to build and use planning performance dashboards — the right metrics, the right views, for the right audience.

planning-horizons.title: Planning Horizons
planning-horizons.description: Long-term, mid-term, and short-term planning horizons — what each covers, who owns it, and how they connect.

planning-logic.title: How the Planning Engine Works
planning-logic.description: How the Planning software planning engine turns network data into optimized, constrained supply plans.

planning-meeting-behaviour.title: Planning Meeting Behaviour
planning-meeting-behaviour.description: What good looks like in planning meetings — preparation, participation, and follow-through.

planning-parameters.title: Planning Parameters
planning-parameters.description: Lead times, lot sizes, safety stock levels, and other parameters that shape how the planning engine calculates.

process-01-scenario-planning-fundamental.title: Scenario Planning Fundamentals
process-01-scenario-planning-fundamental.description: Understand what scenario planning is, when to use it, and how a structured what-if process leads to better planning decisions.

process-02-running-scenarios.title: Running Scenarios in Planning Software
process-02-running-scenarios.description: The practical how-to: creating, adjusting, comparing, and promoting scenarios within the Planning software.

process-03-operating-model.title: Planning Overview
process-03-operating-model.description: Understand the planning operating model: the S&OP and S&OE cycles, execution handoff, and the meeting cadence that keeps plans aligned with reality.

process-04-planning-policy.title: Planning Policy
process-04-planning-policy.description: Planning policies — service level targets, safety stock rules, allocation logic, and prioritisation frameworks — are the documented decisions that govern how the planning system behaves.

process-05-governance-and-escalation.title: Governance & Escalation
process-05-governance-and-escalation.description: Governance defines who decides what in the planning process. Escalation paths, decision frameworks, and exception-based working ensure decisions are made at the right level and at the right time.

process-06-kpis.title: Supply Chain Planning KPIs
process-06-kpis.description: Key performance indicators for supply chain planning — what to measure, how to define it, who owns it, and how to connect KPI performance to the S&OP cycle.

product-lifecycle-planning.description: How new product introductions and phase-outs are planned — NPI process, demand ramp-up, and end-of-life wind-down.

production-planner.description: Role profile for the Production Planner — scheduling, shop floor liaison, and execution feedback.

root-cause-analysis.description: How to diagnose what is driving a KPI — the analytical approach to understanding planning performance.

rough-cut-capacity-planning.description: RCCP — validating the MPS against key resources before committing to the plan.

sales.title: Sales
sales.description: How the Sales function participates in demand review and S&OP — commercial inputs, forecast ownership, and plan commitments.

scenario-assumptions.title: Scenario Assumptions
scenario-assumptions.description: The assumptions that underpin scenarios — demand growth, yield changes, capacity upgrades, and risk events.

soe-01-soe-fundamentals.title: S&OE Fundamentals
soe-01-soe-fundamentals.description: Understand what Sales & Operations Execution is, how it differs from S&OP, and how the weekly cadence keeps supply and demand aligned between monthly planning cycles.

soe-02-running-soe.title: Running S&OE
soe-02-running-soe.description: How to run the weekly S&OE cycle in practice — monitoring performance, resolving exceptions, adjusting the near-term plan, and keeping the handoff to execution clean.

soe-demand-monitoring.title: Demand Monitoring
soe-demand-monitoring.description: Tracking actual demand performance against the agreed plan, identifying deviations early, and escalating where the gap requires a planning response.

soe-integrated-review.title: Integrated S&OE Review
soe-integrated-review.description: The weekly cross-functional review that aligns demand, supply, and operations on the near-term plan — the S&OE equivalent of the executive S&OP meeting.

soe-supply-monitoring.title: Supply Monitoring
soe-supply-monitoring.description: Tracking supply execution against the plan — production output, supplier deliveries, and inventory movements — and flagging constraint-driven deviations before they become service failures.

sop-01-sop-fundamentals.title: S&OP Fundamentals
sop-01-sop-fundamentals.description: Understand what Sales & Operations Planning is, why it exists, who participates, and how it creates a single agreed plan for the business.

sop-demand-forecasting.title: Demand Forecasting
sop-demand-forecasting.description: Turning raw sales history into a single consensus demand number — from data cleansing through statistical modelling, commercial review, and final lock.

sop-inventory-planning.title: Inventory Planning
sop-inventory-planning.description: Setting and maintaining inventory targets that balance service levels against working capital — from coverage analysis through exception resolution.

sop-resource-planning.title: Resource Planning
sop-resource-planning.description: Translating the supply plan into labour, equipment, and capacity requirements — identifying binding constraints and allocating available resource to the plan.

sop-sop-review.title: S&OP Review
sop-sop-review.description: Bringing demand, supply, inventory, and resource plans together — from cross-functional pre-alignment through executive sign-off and plan distribution.

sop-supply-planning.title: Supply Planning
sop-supply-planning.description: Translating the consensus demand plan into a feasible, constrained production and procurement schedule — identifying gaps and preparing costed options for resolution.

stakeholder-alignment.description: How to align stakeholders on planning priorities, assumptions, and decisions before and after key meetings.
